interactions.mechanism: CYP2C9 and CYP3A4 inhibition by amiodarone significantly increases warfarin exposure
interactions.clinical_effect: INR may double or triple; major bleeding risk
interactions.management: Reduce warfarin dose by 30-50% when starting amiodarone. Monitor INR every 3-5 days for several weeks. Amiodarone's effect persists for months after discontinuation.
interactions.onset: Delayed (weeks to months due to amiodarone's long half-life)

interactions.mechanism: Pharmacodynamic: aspirin inhibits platelet aggregation + COX-mediated anticoagulation potentiation
interactions.clinical_effect: Significantly increased bleeding risk, particularly GI bleeding
interactions.management: Avoid combination unless clearly indicated (e.g., mechanical heart valve + AF). If necessary, use lowest aspirin dose (81mg); consider PPI co-prescription.

interactions.mechanism: CYP2C9 inhibition by fluconazole markedly reduces warfarin metabolism
interactions.clinical_effect: INR elevation of 50-100% within 3-5 days; major bleeding risk
interactions.management: Reduce warfarin dose ~25-50%. Check INR 3-5 days after fluconazole initiation and after completion.

interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin is a potent CYP3A4 inhibitor; markedly increases simvastatin plasma levels
interactions.clinical_effect: Up to 10-fold increase in simvastatin AUC; significantly elevated myopathy and rhabdomyolysis risk
interactions.management: CONTRAINDICATED. Hold simvastatin during clarithromycin course. Use azithromycin as alternative when possible. If statin needed, use pravastatin or rosuvastatin (not CYP3A4 substrates).

interactions.clinical_effect: 3-5 fold increase in atorvastatin AUC; increased myopathy risk
interactions.management: Do not exceed atorvastatin 20mg during clarithromycin therapy. Consider switching to azithromycin or temporarily holding atorvastatin.

interactions.mechanism: Serotonergic synergy; tramadol inhibits serotonin reuptake AND is metabolized by CYP2D6
interactions.clinical_effect: Risk of serotonin syndrome (fever, agitation, myoclonus, diarrhea, tachycardia, diaphoresis)
interactions.management: Avoid combination. If pain management needed, consider non-serotonergic alternatives. If used, counsel patient on serotonin syndrome symptoms; start with low tramadol dose.

interactions.mechanism: Dual serotonergic mechanism; citalopram + tramadol both increase serotonergic tone
interactions.clinical_effect: Serotonin syndrome risk; QT prolongation risk (citalopram already prolongs QT)
interactions.management: Avoid. Use alternative analgesics. If combination unavoidable, monitor closely for serotonin syndrome and QTc prolongation.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce prostaglandin-mediated vasodilation, counteracting ACE inhibitor antihypertensive effect; combined renal effects increase AKI risk
interactions.clinical_effect: Reduced antihypertensive efficacy; increased risk of acute kidney injury (especially in elderly, dehydrated, or CKD patients)
interactions.management: Avoid chronic NSAID use with ACE inhibitors. For acute pain, use acetaminophen preferentially. Monitor BP and renal function if NSAIDs necessary.

interactions.mechanism: Dual mechanism potassium retention: ACE inhibitor reduces aldosterone, spironolactone blocks aldosterone receptor
interactions.clinical_effect: Significant hyperkalemia risk, particularly in CKD, diabetes, or elderly patients
interactions.management: Monitor potassium and renal function at baseline, 1 week after starting, and periodically. Avoid combination if K+ >5.0 mEq/L or eGFR <30. Dose adjustment often needed.

interactions.mechanism: Furosemide can increase metformin plasma levels by reducing renal clearance; diuretic-induced dehydration reduces GFR
interactions.clinical_effect: Increased metformin levels; if dehydration + reduced GFR, lactic acidosis risk
interactions.management: Monitor renal function during diuretic initiation or dose changes. Hold metformin if acute illness causing dehydration. Ensure adequate hydration.

interactions.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits P-glycoprotein and CYP3A4, reducing digoxin clearance
interactions.clinical_effect: Digoxin toxicity: bradycardia, heart block, nausea, visual disturbances, arrhythmias
interactions.management: Reduce digoxin dose by 50% when starting amiodarone. Monitor digoxin levels and heart rate/rhythm closely.

interactions.mechanism: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, which activates clopidogrel to its active metabolite
interactions.clinical_effect: Reduced antiplatelet effect of clopidogrel; potential increase in cardiovascular events (clinical significance debated)
interactions.management: Use pantoprazole or famotidine as alternatives if GI protection needed with clopidogrel. FDA has issued safety communication about this interaction.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal prostaglandin synthesis, decreasing lithium clearance
interactions.clinical_effect: Lithium toxicity: tremor, confusion, ataxia, seizures, cardiac arrhythmias
interactions.management: Avoid NSAIDs with lithium. Use acetaminophen for pain. If NSAID unavoidable, monitor lithium levels within 5 days and adjust dose.

interactions.mechanism: Metronidazole inhibits CYP2C9, reducing warfarin metabolism; also inhibits gut flora reducing vitamin K production
interactions.clinical_effect: INR elevation of 30-50%; significant bleeding risk
interactions.management: Reduce warfarin dose ~25%. Monitor INR 3-5 days after starting and after completing metronidazole course.

interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein, increasing apixaban exposure
interactions.clinical_effect: Increased apixaban levels ~60%; increased bleeding risk
interactions.management: Use with caution. Consider using azithromycin instead of clarithromycin. No dose adjustment specified in labeling, but monitor for bleeding signs.
